scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and is competent to institute this suit.
2. The Defendant is a person sui juris and can be sued in this Court.
3. The Defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court, invoking Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
4. The suit value is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court, and this Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

LIMITATION

5. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, as the loan was repayable on demand and the suit is filed within three years from the date of demand and default.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

6. The Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant on 15.03.2024.
7. The said amount was transferred via NEFT bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by the bank transaction record annexed as Annexure A.
8. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount via WhatsApp messages, copies of which are annexed as Annexure B.
9. The loan was advanced with the understanding that the Defendant would repay the same on demand or within a reasonable time.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

10. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the Plaintiff's repeated oral demands.
11. The Defendant committed default in repayment on 20.06.2024, which is treated as the date of default for the purpose of interest calculation.
12. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment, as per Annexure C.
13. The Defendant has failed to respond to the legal notice and has not made any repayment towards the outstanding dues.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

14. The principal amount due from the Defendant is Rs. 15,00,000.
15. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation.
16. The interest claimed is simple interest and not compound interest, as there is no agreement for compound interest.
17. The total claim amount comprises the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 plus accrued interest calculated at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024.

LEGAL BASIS

18. The Plaintiff is entitled to recovery under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant has breached the obligation to repay the loan.
19. The loan was advanced for lawful consideration via bank transfer, pre-empting any defence regarding lack of consideration.
20. The suit is within limitation as the Defendant acknowledged the debt via WhatsApp messages and the legal notice was sent within the statutory period.
21. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the rate claimed is within market norms for unsecured lending.
22. This Court has the authority to adjudicate this dispute under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

23. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite demand.
24. The cause of action further arose on 01.07.2024 when the legal notice was sent and the Defendant failed to comply.
25. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under the Limitation Act, 1963.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

26. The suit is valued at {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}} for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee.
27. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid under the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.
28. The plaint is properly stamped and tendered with the requisite court fee as per law.

INTEREST

29. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:
    (a) Pendente lite interest from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree;
    (b) Future interest from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

PRAYER

30. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
(a) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000 towards principal amount in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;
(b) Award interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation on the principal amount;
(c) Award future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit
